forecasts.reasoning: As of June 15, 2025, there are no scheduled dates for Donald Trump to visit China before the July 1 deadline. While Trump indicated on June 5 that he accepted an invitation from Xi Jinping, a presidential visit typically requires extensive logistical and diplomatic preparation spanning several weeks or months. Furthermore, Trump is attending the G7 Summit in Canada from June 15-17, leaving an exceptionally narrow 13-day window to complete a major bilateral visit to Beijing before July 1. Without any White House announcement or reports of advance teams as of June 15, the likelihood of a visit materializing in the back half of June is exceedingly low.
